home.moodPlaceholder: 🤖 Tell the AI your mood… e.g. “cold day, want soup”

draw.locationDenied: Location was denied — pick a spot in the filters instead.
draw.locationFailed: Couldn't get your location — pick a spot in the filters instead.
draw.keyInvalid: Your Google key was rejected — check it in Settings.
draw.quotaReached: Daily quota reached — the cap did its job protecting your wallet. Try tomorrow!
draw.exhausted: You re-spun through every match! Loosen a filter and draw again.

conditions.title: Draw conditions
conditions.cuisineHint: Tap once to include ✓, twice to exclude ✕, three times to clear.
conditions.budgetHint: Places without price data still appear, marked “price n/a”.

history.empty: No draws yet. Your picks will appear here.

pwa.iosHint: In Safari: tap the Share button, then “Add to Home Screen” for the full app experience.

google.title: Google Places
google.active: Key saved on this device — live restaurant data is on.
google.inactive: No key yet — the app runs on demo data.

ai.title: AI concierge (optional)
ai.note: Any OpenAI-compatible endpoint. Everything stays on this device; requests go straight from your browser to the endpoint you set. Configured = a mood box appears on the draw screen and result cards get a one-line blurb.
ai.loadError: Couldn't load models — check base URL and key.

notif.title: Meal reminders
notif.intro: A nudge when it's time to decide — pick the meals, weekdays and times. Times follow this device's clock ({tz}).
notif.privacy: Only your push address and this schedule are stored on the notification server — never your keys, location, or history.
notif.denied: Notifications are blocked for this site. Allow them in your browser or system settings, then come back.
notif.iosInstall: On iPhone/iPad, install the app first (Share → Add to Home Screen), then enable notifications from inside the installed app.
notif.unsupported: This browser doesn't support web push notifications.
notif.serverError: Couldn't reach the notification server — try again in a moment.
notif.testFailed: Couldn't send — try turning notifications off and on again.

blocklist.title: Never-suggest list
blocklist.empty: Empty — block places from their result card.

data.title: Your data
data.note: Everything lives only on this device. iOS may evict browser data after ~7 days of not using the site — export a backup if your history matters.
data.confirmClear: Delete all draw history? This cannot be undone.

setup.title: Set up in 5 minutes
setup.intro: EatWhat uses your own free Google key for live restaurant data. It stays on your device, and a quota cap you control means your card can never be surprise-billed. No key? Demo mode still works.
setup.projectT: 1 · Create a Google Cloud project & enable “Places API (New)”
setup.projectD: Sign in to Google Cloud Console, create a project (billing must be attached — the free monthly quota covers personal use), then enable exactly “Places API (New)” — not the legacy “Places API”.
setup.restrictT: 2 · Create an API key and restrict it
setup.restrictD: Credentials → Create credentials → API key. Then edit it: Application restrictions → Websites → add {origin} · API restrictions → Places API (New) only.
setup.capT: 3 · Set daily quota caps — secure your $0 bill
setup.capD: Google only lets you edit quotas after the account is upgraded from “Free Trial” to pay-as-you-go — click the blue Upgrade button at the top right of the console. Don't worry: you keep every monthly free allowance, and with the caps below the bill physically cannot leave $0.
setup.capHow: In the console open Google Maps Platform → Quotas and set the dropdown to Places API (New). For each request type below: tick its checkbox → Edit Quota → enter the value → Submit.
setup.capPerMinute: Optional extra brake — also set the per-minute limits so nobody can burn a whole day's quota in one spree: {caps}.

quota.searchNearby: Each draw costs at most 1 search (results are cached for 24 h). 30 × 31 days stays inside even the strictest 1,000/month free tier.
quota.photo: Photos load only for the winning restaurant. 30/day keeps the photo bucket free forever.
quota.getPlace: Only used when you pick a custom centre point on the map.
quota.searchText: Only used by the free key-validation call — a low cap just blunts abuse.
quota.autocomplete: Location-typing suggestions. Sessions end with a details call, which makes them free; 300/day is plenty.

setup.keyD: Validation uses a free call — it never costs anything. The key is stored only in this browser.
setup.keyValid: Key works — live data is on!
setup.keyInvalid: Key rejected. Check the API restriction includes Places API (New) and this site's origin is allowed.
setup.locD: Used to find restaurants around you. You can always pick a spot manually instead.
setup.locDenied: Denied — you can pick an origin manually in the draw filters.
